content.claim: The number of children identified with special educational needs (SNI) in Hungary exceeded 69,000 in the 2025/2026 school year according to KSH (Central Statistical Office) data, with a broader count across all education levels reaching nearly 120,000.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Different sources use varying denominators (general-school-only vs. full system), making cross-source comparisons problematic
The 69,000 vs. 120,000 discrepancy reflects definitional scope rather than measurement error, requiring clarification of which population serves planning purposes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
